metadata.source: The Five Governance Gates section, Paper1_Manuscript.docx
metadata.description: Five governance gate definitions with evidence floors and overridable status
metadata.note: The five-domain decomposition reflects a convergence of requirements across the regulatory instruments reviewed.

minimum_evidence_floor: External or temporal validation relevant to the deployment population [9]
Defined statement of intended use and clinical actionability
gates.rationale: A system without evidence of deployment-context performance cannot be safely activated under any documented rationale.

minimum_evidence_floor: Subgroup performance evaluation across socially salient characteristics [12]
Defined governance response for detected inequities
gates.rationale: Risk that a system with acceptable aggregate performance produces systematically worse outcomes for specific subpopulations.

minimum_evidence_floor: Model card or model facts label describing data provenance, exclusion criteria, and known risks [15,16]
gates.rationale: Documentation fulfils both operational and transparency functions.

minimum_evidence_floor: Named accountable owner with explicit suspension authority
Separation between procurement incentives and deployment authorisation [18]
Predefined metrics covering performance, calibration, and alert fatigue
Prespecified thresholds triggering formal review, pause, or withdrawal
gates.rationale: A deployed system that cannot be monitored cannot be safely recalled when performance deteriorates.
